div1.entryFree:
Murmur, vb. 1) to give a low and indistinct sound: the --ing stream, As IV, 3, 80. the --ing surge, Lr. IV, 6, 20. trans.: the fresh streams ran by her and --ed her moans, Oth. IV, 3, 45.
2) to speak to one's self and indistinctly: --ing 'Where's my serpent of old Nile'? Ant. I, 5, 25. trans.: I heard thee m. tales of iron wars, H4A II, 3, 51.
3) to grumble: if thou more --est, Tp. I, 2, 294. the --ing lips of discontent, John IV, 2, 53.
